title: Implementação de Fractal em FPGA Usando o Processador Soft-Core SAPHO
journal: Encontro Nacional de Modelagem Computacional— ENMC 2025
abstract: O uso de fractais no processamento e na análise de dados tem ganhado destaque em diversas áreas científicas e tecnológicas. Neste trabalho, é apresentada a implementação do fractal do Conjunto de Mandelbrot utilizando o processador SAPHO em FPGA, explorando sua arquitetura reconfigurável para viabilizar o processamento rápido e eficiente de imagens fractais em tempo real. Além disso, propõem-se otimizações no código Assembly gerado para o SAPHO, que resultam em um ganho de desempenho de aproximadamente 25% em relação à implementação padrão.

title: An Image Acquisition System Based on State Machine and Sampling ADCs
journal: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: The present work reports on the development of a digital system for image acquisition which is able to process two electric signals of amplitude varying between 0 and 10 V. The system correlates both signals in a two-dimensional (2-D) histogram. and coordinates for every event are obtained from the amplitudes of two coincident signals. The hardware consists of two analog-to-digital converters (ADCs), two complex programmable logic devices (CPLDs), and one 2 MB static random access memory (SRAM), implemented in a card that is plugged into personal computers. The data acquisition rate may be as high as 1.0 106 events per second, and does not depend on the PC processor. The software code has been written in the Delphi environment using Assembly routines for the I/O bus operations. Image sizes may be chosen from 128 128 up to 1024 1024 pixels and may be viewed in color scale, with two or three-dimen-sional graphics. Images are shown to illustrate the applicability to two-dimensional position sensitive X-ray detectors.

title: A TDC-Based System for X-Ray Imaging Detectors
authors: A. F. Barbosa, H. P. Lima, Jr.
journal: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: An image acquisition system is presented for use with position sensitive detectors (PSD). The system is based on a high-resolution time-to-digital converter (TDC) and a field programmable gate array (FPGA). The detectors use gas as absorbing medium and two delay lines to identify the coordinates of each detected particle. The TDC translates the time information coming from the delay lines into digital words, from which the particle position coordinates are encoded. The FPGA is responsible for processing each event, controlling the data acquisition and communicating with a personal computer. Images of 256 × 256 pixels are stored in an on-board memory, and this resolution is increased to 512 × 512 pixels by using a time multiplexing technique. The maximum data acquisition rate is 1.2 million events per second. X-ray images obtained with the system are shown, illustrating the overall performance.

title: Production and Quality Control of MWPC for the LHCb Muon System at CERN
journal: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: This work describes the production and quality control of the multi-wire proportional chamber for the LHCb muon detection system. The European Laboratory of Particle Physics (CERN) is in charge of the production of 134 chambers for the inner regions of the five Muon Stations. These chambers have demanding requirements on rate capability ( 100 kHz/cm2) and efficiency ( 99%) in a short time window (20 ns), on overall response uniformity which has to be better than 30%, and on the aging properties. The technical design of the chambers is briefly presented, and the production line is described together with the quality assurance and quality control programs. This includes fabrication of the panels; wire winding, gluing, and soldering; gap uniformity control; pitch and wire tension measurement; gas leak tests; high-voltage training; and tests under intense radiation. An original method developed for gas gain uniformity measurements is described in detail, and the obtained results are shown.

title: Gas gain uniformity tests performed on multiwire proportional chambers for the LHCb muon system
journal: Nuclear Instruments and Methods in Physics Research
abstract: We present the experimental setup and the results of the gas gain uniformity tests performed as part of the quality control of the multiwire proportional chambers produced at CERN for the LHCb muon system. The test provides a relative gas gain measurement over the whole chamber sensitive area. It is based on the analysis of the pulse height spectrum obtained when the chamber is exposed to a Am radioactive source. Since the measurement is normalized to the peak of a precise pulse generator, the gain uniformity can also be evaluated among different gas gaps and different chambers. In order to cope with the specific requirements related to the relatively high number of chambers and to their varying geometry, a standalone and compact data acquisition system has been developed which is programmable at the hardware level and may be applied to many other applications requiring precise time-to-digital and analog-to-digital conversion, in correlated or non-correlated mode.

title: Three-dimensional event visualization for the ATLAS calorimeter
journal: Computer Physics Communications
abstract: The ATLAS detector was commissioned with cosmic rays. For such commissioning, a number of software tools has been developed to support data analysis. Among ATLAS sub-detectors, commissioning the calorimeter system demanded a considerable effort due to its segmentation into seven detection layers, which produce more than a hundred thousand readout channels. Tasks like performance evaluation of the calorimeter, calibration and handling noisy or dead channels benefit a lot from cosmic muon track visualization, which facilitates the identification of the activated cells in the calorimeter. The coherence of the reconstructed data can be visually checked and potential problems can be detected in a easier way. This work presents a 3D visualization tool for the ATLAS calorimeter system, the CaloGeoView. The tool was built using ROOT Framework, which provides a smooth integration with analyses currently performed by the ATLAS community. The CaloGeoView structure and some applications with reconstructed data are presented. Due to its 3D graphical interface, the CaloGeoView has been providing a simple and intuitive way to analyze results of event reconstruction.

title: Calorimeter Response Deconvolution for Energy Estimation in High-Luminosity Conditions
journal: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Signal superposition, also called pile-up noise, has become an important issue for calorimeters operating under high-luminosity conditions. Current techniques model those superimposed signals as highly correlated noise, whose covariance matrix information is used to develop a minimal variance and unbiased filter for energy estimation. Hence, the filter coefficients depend on the current luminosity. This paper presents a new energy estimator whose design is independent of the luminosity. The method consists of deconvolving the front-end electronic shaping response, recovering the primary impulse signals. The deconvolution process allows the identification of each superimposed component. Based on that information, it is possible to include additional constraints in order to design orthogonal estimators for each component, providing optimal estimators suitable to operate in colliders where luminosity changes during the run. Comparisons between current approaches and the proposed method using pile-up event simulation are presented. Results point out that an improvement on amplitude estimation performance is achieved and stable signal reconstruction is provided for different luminosity ranges.

title: An implementation of a Power System Smart Waveform Recorder using FPGA and ARM cores
journal: Measurement
abstract: In this paper, the design and the prototype implementation of a Power Quality (PQ) disturbance detector and compressor are described. This instrument, named Power System Smart Waveform Recorder (PSSWR), is able to acquire samples of Electric Power System (EPS) signals and process them to detect, compress, and store disturbance waveforms on an SD card, from which they can be reconstructed and analyzed offline using a suitable computer application. The prototype uses, among other devices, Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) and ARM platforms to process EPS signals in an intelligent way. The PSSWR is capable of detecting and recording both well-known PQ disturbance waveforms and new disturbances not yet observed in EPS, thanks to the Novelty Detection concept. This characteristic is important in the context of smart grids, where hidden disturbances can be identified using this methodology.

title: Gapless Power-Quality Disturbance Recorder
journal: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: This paper presents and discusses the concepts of a gapless power quality disturbance recorder (G-PQDR) and a novelty detector. In the G-PQDR, power signals are divided into continuous frames, and the energy difference between a current frame and a reference frame is used for comparison. If the current frame differs from the reference frame, it is recorded and becomes the new reference; otherwise, the frame is discarded and only the frequency of the fundamental component is stored. The G-PQDR is capable of detecting and recording both previously known PQ disturbance waveforms and those not yet observed in power systems, thanks to the novelty detection concept. This feature is particularly important in the context of smart grids, where hidden disturbances can be identified using this methodology. To ensure efficient data storage by minimizing the volume of stored data, the G-PQDR employs both lossy and lossless compression techniques. This paper describes the complete process of detection and compression and compares the performance of the proposed method with that of a commercial power quality data recorder. Additionally, details of the prototype implementation are presented.

title: Sparse Representation for Signal Reconstruction in Calorimeters Operating in High Luminosity
journal: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: A calorimeter signal reconstruction method based on sparse representation (SR) of redundant data is proposed for energy reconstruction in particle colliders operating under high-luminosity conditions. Signal overlapping is initially modeled as an underdetermined linear system, leading to a convex set of feasible solutions. The solution with the smallest number of superimposed signals (i.e., the sparse representation) that best represents the recorded data is obtained using an interior-point (IP) optimization procedure. From a signal processing perspective, this procedure performs source separation, enabling the estimation of the amplitude of each convoluted signal. Simulation results include a comparison between the proposed method and a standard signal reconstruction approach. For this purpose, a toy Monte Carlo simulation was developed, focusing exclusively on calorimeter front-end signal generation, where different levels of pile-up and signal-to-noise ratio were considered to evaluate the method. The results indicate that the proposed approach can be competitive in high-luminosity environments.

title: Embedded Signal Processing Module for Online Filtering in High-Event Rate Conditions
journal: Journal of Communication and Information Systems
abstract: Online event detection (filtering) is required in communications, industry, and electronic instrumentation systems, which may involve sequential decision levels. In some cases, the decision to reject or accept an event relies on the fusion of different measured data. This work describes an embedded solution implemented on a field-programmable gate array (FPGA), capable of combining information from two different sources to support decision-making. A matched filter discriminator is employed to identify signatures of interest. The main focus is on digital data control, packaging module design, and the implementation and optimization of the matched filter module in terms of FPGA resource utilization. The proposed digital electronic system is presented, and simulation results are used to validate the design.

title: Online energy reconstruction for calorimeters under high pile-up conditions using deconvolutional techniques
journal: Journal of Instrumentation
abstract: High-energy calorimeters operating at high data acquisition rates may suffer from signal superposition (pile-up) in their electronic readout. This issue arises when the time interval between two consecutive incoming physics signals is shorter than the front-end electronics latency. Signal pile-up can significantly degrade online energy estimation, and some palliative approaches treat pile-up as an additional noise source to preserve the well-established Optimal Filter theory as the baseline for signal estimation. To enable actual online compensation for pile-up effects, the method proposed in this paper models the front-end electronics as a linear transmission channel whose transfer function can be equalized (deconvolved). For experiments in which the acquisition rate is synchronized with the collision clock, the equalizer output allows direct energy estimation per bunch-crossing (BC). Optimal approximations for the equalizer design are proposed using both FIR (Finite Impulse Response) filters and an iterative method. The latter provides improved reconstruction performance (i.e., smaller errors), but at the cost of higher computational complexity and additional data-flow circuitry for online implementation. Toy simulation data, based on typical calorimeter front-end responses, are used to evaluate the performance of the proposed methods.

title: Sparse representation algorithm applied to power systems signal compression
journal: International Journal of Numerical Modelling: Electronic Networks, Devices and Fields
abstract: Power system signal compression is an important task due to the nature of electrical disturbances, which must be analyzed offline to extract useful information. This paper presents a comparative study of greedy algorithms for obtaining sparse representations of signals applied to power system signal compression. The algorithms are evaluated based on two parameters: the quality of the compressed signal, measured by its correlation coefficient relative to the original signal, and the number of elements in the representation, which is related to the compression ratio. Additionally, computational complexity, in terms of required floating-point operations (FLOPs), is analyzed to identify which algorithm is most suitable for real-time implementation. Finally, a comparison with the wavelet transform is performed using a database of real power system signals.

title: Performance Evaluation of Energy Reconstruction Methods in High Energy Physics Experiments
journal: Mundi
abstract: The discovery of particles that shape our universe drives the scientific community to develop increasingly sophisticated equipment. Particle accelerators are complex machines that bring particle beams into collision at speeds close to the speed of light. When these collisions occur, secondary products are generated and measured by calorimeter systems, which fully absorb them. Typically, high-energy calorimeters are highly segmented, comprising thousands of dedicated readout channels. This work evaluates the performance of two energy reconstruction algorithms: the Optimal Filter (OF) and the Multi-Amplitude Estimator (MAE), recently proposed to address signal superposition (pile-up). To assess energy estimation efficiency, artificial data are used under various pile-up conditions. Statistical analysis of the estimated energy is then employed to compare the performance of each method. A second analysis evaluates the sensitivity of the MAE to the pedestal parameter. The results show that the MAE method outperforms the OF method, although the use of an uncalibrated pedestal value negatively affects its performance.

title: A matched filter based approach for high-energy estimation in calorimetry
journal: Journal of Instrumentation
abstract: In high-energy calorimeters, a crucial task is the reconstruction of the energy deposited by particle interactions. Standard techniques used in modern calorimeters rely on energy estimation to select signals with relevant information. This work presents a new approach that performs signal detection in the presence of noise as a first step, followed by energy estimation. The method is fully based on Matched Filter (MF) theory, which is known to provide optimal detection efficiency with respect to the signal-to-noise ratio. Furthermore, the MF output can be calibrated to estimate the signal amplitude and, consequently, the energy. The proposed method is compared with different optimal filtering algorithms currently used for energy reconstruction in modern calorimeter systems. Simulation results show that the proposed approach achieves superior performance in terms of both signal detection efficiency and estimation error.

title: Linear Equation System Solver Circuit for Streaming Processing in FPGA
journal: Journal of Control, Automation and Electrical Systems
abstract: The solution of linear equation systems (LES) has numerous applications in digital signal processing. However, due to its high computational cost, implementing such solutions in high-speed, free-running (streaming) processing applications is challenging. This work presents an FPGA-based implementation of a digital circuit for solving LES using the gradient descent method, capable of operating online. This implementation introduces a new perspective for executing iterative, matrix-based algorithms in embedded systems designed for high-speed (tens of MHz) continuous data flow. In other words, the proposed method functions as a real-time processing block, where new data enters and processed data exits at each clock cycle, enabling seamless integration of matrix computations into continuous-flow systems. In addition to presenting the implementation architecture, this work analyzes the relationship between the number of iterations, resource utilization, and the achievable acquisition rate. As a case study, the method is applied to a channel equalization problem.

title: Sparse deconvolution methods for online energy estimation in calorimeters operating in high luminosity conditions
journal: Journal of Instrumentation
abstract: Energy reconstruction in calorimeters operating in high-luminosity particle colliders has become a significant challenge. In this scenario, pulses from the calorimeter front-end output overlap (pile-up effect), compromising energy estimation when no preprocessing for signal separation is applied. Recently, signal deconvolution-based methods have been proposed for both online and offline reconstruction. For online processing, constraints such as processing speed, memory requirements, and implementation cost limit overall performance. In contrast, offline reconstruction allows the use of Sparse Representation theory to implement more sophisticated iterative deconvolution methods. This paper presents iterative deconvolution approaches based on sparse representation algorithms with computational costs suitable for online implementation. Using simulated data, the proposed methods are compared with current techniques to evaluate performance in online environments. The analysis shows that, despite higher computational cost compared to standard methods, the performance gains may justify their use—particularly the Separable Surrogate Functional approach, which proves to be feasible for implementation on modern FPGAs.

title: Spectral Variation-Based Signal Compression Technique for Gapless Power Quality Waveform Recording in Smart Grids
journal: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: The current scenario of electric power systems expansion requires the insertion of new devices and technologies, contributing to the establishment of smart grids. However, the more instruments are connected to the grid, the greater the amount of data resulting from the measurements, which can cause a bottleneck in data transferring. The present article addresses this problem by proposing a system that implements a novel and efficient real-time data compression method based on the power signal spectral content variation, focused on long-term gapless oscillographic analysis. This system also provides a high-fidelity reconstruction for full oscillography power quality analysis. Comparison results with synthetic and real signals using another gapless power quality disturbance recorder present in literature and a commercial power quality disturbance recorder show that the proposed technique is capable to achieve a compression rate more than ten times greater than both while providing a better reconstruction quality, reaching compression ratio values of up to 10780:1 for synthetic signals and 9470:1 for real signals.

title: Identification of Low Impedance Points Along Railway Tracks From a Railroad Inspection Vehicle
journal: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This work addresses the subject of preventive maintenance of railroads which has a considerable impact on the cost and safety of railway companies by proposing a new technique to detect concentrated regions of low impedance between rails along railway lines from a railroad inspection vehicle. Its methodology is based on the standing waves theory applied to transmission lines. Simulation and field measurements have been carried out in order to understand and validate such technique, and a compact measurement system has been designed and implemented producing field-testing results that clearly indicate its feasibility and effectiveness.

title: Performance of Optimal Linear Filtering Methods for Signal Estimation in High-Energy Calorimetry
journal: Journal of Control, Automation and Electrical Systems
abstract: Discrete linear estimation methods, which are based on a weighted sum of the received time samples and the filter coefficients, are extensively employed in many applications. In conditions where the expected pulse shape is known and the received signal is mainly corrupted from additive Gaussian noise, such approaches perform close to the optimal operation. However, the readout signal may also suffer from pile-up, which potentially deteriorates the estimation process. In this paper, signal estimation is carried out for high-event rate experiments in high-energy physics, which aim at reconstructing particle collisions. In particular, the energies of the subproducts resulting from the collisions are measured by the calorimeter systems using shaped signals and estimating their amplitudes. The paper presents four linear estimation approaches and compares their performance in high signal pile-up conditions: two versions of an Optimal Filter that minimizes the noise variance impacts, a Wiener Filter to cope with the uncertainties arising from both the noise and the signal fluctuations, and a signal deconvolution technique that recovers the original signals within a calorimeter readout window. The efficiency is measured from simulated data where various signal pile-up scenarios and noise conditions are considered.

title: Probability based data quality for energy reconstruction in high energy calorimetry
journal: The Journal of Engineering and Exact Sciences
abstract: In several engineering applications, the quality of the reconstruction of the information of interest directly interferes with the success of decision making. In modern high-energy physics experiments, the reconstruction of events is essential for the precise observation of particles and important discoveries for science. In this context, the signals from the calorimetry system provide information about the energy of the particles produced in collisions. Typically, the energy reconstruction quality is measured using the χ² method. However, in experiments operating in high luminosity conditions, such an approach is not able to operate under signals pile-up conditions, being only able to detect the presence of pile-up effect in the received signal. Therefore, in this work, a new approach to evaluate the quality of energy reconstruction under pile-up conditions is presented. The proposed method relies on the calculation of probabilities regardless of the energy reconstruction algorithm employed. Using simulated data for a general calorimeter system operating under different signals pile-up conditions, the results show that the use of the proposed method increases signals selection efficiency.

title: An FPGA-based parallel deconvolution application envisaging high-energy calorimeters operating under severe pile-up conditions
journal: Journal of Instrumentation
abstract: Signal pileup may arise when the system response time is larger than what would be required by the event rate in the application. In this condition, information superposition occurs, and signal estimation is deteriorated due to such information distortion. For online applications, mitigating such a pileup distortion usually requires embedded digital signal processing, which often involves FPGA-based designs when high event rates are a concern. Recently, high-energy particle experiments started facing an unprecedented increase in pileup conditions, which demanded new approaches for signal estimation. This work proposes a dedicated parallel signal processing system embedded on an FPGA platform that implements different filtering techniques for online energy reconstruction in calorimeters (energy measurement) operating under severe pileup conditions. Each designed filter is an inverse approximation of the calorimeter readout channel’s impulse response, performing deconvolution of the incoming signals. Such a deconvolution approach for energy estimation was recently introduced, and the proposed filters were implemented in this work. Evaluation of the digital system design was performed using a data set that considers a typical high-energy calorimeter front-end response, as high-luminosity particle collider experiments produce high pileup distortions in calorimeter signals when processing the subproducts of the collisions. As the Large Hadron Collider (LHC) provides the most demanding conditions in terms of signal pileup, the acquisition system of the proposed solution is synchronous with its collision rate (40 MHz). The results showed that the proposed implementation may be applied as a preprocessing (pileup reduction) step in calorimeter instrumentation and could reduce the signal pileup within the fast response time required by such experiments.

title: Non-negative sparse deconvolution method for PMT signals in radiation detectors
journal: Nuclear Instruments and Methods in Physics Research
abstract: Electrical signals generated by radiation detectors are often affected by undesired effects that can degrade the detector’s energy resolution. These effects may originate from the measurement system itself or from high event rates. Traditional signal shaping and mitigation methods present limitations, such as the need for explicit prior information about the signals. This paper proposes a deconvolution method based on least squares filtering and non-negative sparsity theory to overcome these limitations. The method is evaluated using simulated data representing a gamma-ray detector composed of a NaI(Tl) crystal and a photomultiplier tube (PMT). In this system, the electrical pulses are preprocessed using a CR coupling circuit followed by an RC integrator. The results show that the traditional method achieves an energy resolution of 6.60%, whereas the proposed method improves it to 5.65%. In addition to increasing accuracy, the proposed approach also reduces implementation complexity.

title: A Maximum Likelihood Approach for Signal Estimation and Data Quality Assessment for High-Energy Calorimeter Systems
journal: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: In high-energy calorimetry, energy estimation is typically performed using digital linear filters, from which the amplitude of a conditioned signal is inferred. The linear approach is justified when electronic noise can be approximated by a Gaussian distribution. However, calorimeters operating at high event rates and under high-luminosity conditions—such as those in modern particle colliders—are affected by non-Gaussian noise sources, notably signal tails from adjacent collisions that do not carry relevant physical information about the event of interest. Despite this, the true noise probability density function (pdf) is often neglected, and only second-order statistics are considered to preserve the linear model.This work presents a novel approach based on the full characterization of the noise distribution using maximum likelihood estimators (MLE). An iterative optimization algorithm is proposed to determine the parameter values that maximize a non-Gaussian multivariate distribution. Additionally, the study evaluates the use of maximum likelihood estimates for data quality assessment, enabling the rejection of signals with poor estimation quality. Simulation results comparing the proposed method with conventional linear approaches show that the non-Gaussian MLE achieves superior performance in terms of estimation error across a wide range of cell occupancy conditions. Furthermore, the final likelihood value can be used as a performance metric for signal reconstruction, providing a robust quality factor (QF) for discriminating signals in physics analyses when compared to traditional strategies.

title: Online Pulse Compensation for Energy Spectrum Determination: A Pole-Zero Cancellation and Unfolding Approach
journal: Electronics
abstract: Signal conditioning circuits in particle energy spectrum determination systems introduce shaping characteristics that affect pulse integrity. This study investigates algorithms to compensate for these effects, focusing on digital signal processing techniques for pole-zero cancellation (PZC) and unfolding. The PZC algorithm effectively corrects baseline shifts and pulse amplitude loss, significantly improving signal fidelity. Although the application of digital PZC in streaming mode for high event rates was previously considered impractical, this work proposes an FPGA-based implementation combined with the unfolding method, enabling online compensation and improved performance under various experimental conditions.

title: Learned Soft-Thresholding for Streaming Sparse Signal Recovery in Nuclear Instrumentation
journal: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Experiments in High Energy Physics, such as those conducted at the Large Hadron Collider (LHC), use calorimeters for particle detection and energy measurement. Under high-luminosity conditions, these systems face the pile-up effect, where overlapping signals distort energy estimation and reduce trigger efficiency. Sparse representation (SR)-based methods have been widely explored for signal deconvolution and pile-up mitigation in calorimeters. This paper proposes a novel deconvolution method that combines a finite impulse response (FIR) filter with sparse representation using a dynamic threshold estimated by a neural network. Unlike conventional SR approaches that rely on iterative algorithms—often unsuitable for real-time applications—the proposed method operates in a non-iterative, feed-forward manner, making it compatible with online data acquisition (DAQ) systems. Performance evaluations against traditional least squares (LS) techniques and SR methods with fixed thresholds demonstrate improved accuracy and resolution. Simulated datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach, showing enhanced signal reconstruction while maintaining feasibility for hardware implementation.

title: Multi-Memory Approach for Random Number Generators in FPGA
journal: Applied Sciences
abstract: Random number generation is essential in many application domains, including high-energy physics simulations. Implementing Monte Carlo methods that generate samples according to a desired probability distribution is particularly challenging on hardware platforms such as FPGAs. Direct implementations of analytical distribution functions are often resource-intensive, making them impractical for real-time systems. An efficient alternative is the use of the inverse cumulative distribution function (CDF), which can be implemented using look-up tables (LUTs). In this approach, a uniformly distributed random number—generated by linear feedback shift registers (LFSRs)—is used as an address to access LUTs containing discretized values of the CDF, thereby producing the desired random variable. However, this method presents limited accuracy in low-probability regions of the distribution. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a segmented CDF implementation based on multiple LUTs, improving resolution in poorly sampled regions. A cascade of decision logic is used to select the appropriate memory output, increasing resolution only where necessary while optimizing memory usage. The proposed method is validated through Monte Carlo simulations in particle physics applications, showing close agreement with theoretical distributions while requiring low FPGA resource utilization and no DSP blocks.

title: SAPHO—Scalable-Architecture Processor for Hardware Optimization: An FPGA Customizable Implementation Approach
journal: IEEE Access
abstract: Soft-core processors are increasingly used in field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs) to implement complex functions at critical points in embedded systems, where a pure firmware solution would require excessive development effort or hardware resources. However, most existing processors rely on fixed architectures, allocating the same amount of logic resources regardless of application requirements. This often leads to hardware over-provisioning, oversized data word lengths, and unnecessary power consumption. To address these limitations, this work presents SAPHO, a self-scalable processor that automatically adjusts its program and data memories, as well as its arithmetic and logic circuits, according to software requirements. This design paradigm reduces hardware waste, lowers logic element utilization, and enables more energy-efficient execution compared to conventional soft-core processors. Additionally, SAPHO incorporates a combinational Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU), configurable for fixed- or floating-point operations, ensuring the execution of one instruction per clock cycle without pipeline stalls. Performance evaluation shows that, compared to the widely used commercial soft-core processor NIOS II, SAPHO achieves lower hardware cost and shorter execution time while maintaining scalability and flexibility for different embedded applications. Experimental results indicate that SAPHO executes all arithmetic operations in a fixed sequence of 13 clock cycles, whereas equivalent operations on the NIOS II may require from tens to thousands of cycles depending on configuration and data type. In an 8-point FFT benchmark, SAPHO reduces execution time by 77.36% compared to the fastest NIOS II configuration. Furthermore, SAPHO requires fewer logic elements (LEs) and demonstrates lower dynamic core power consumption, with reductions ranging from 15% to 33% compared to NIOS II variants. These results confirm that SAPHO provides a more efficient solution in terms of hardware cost, performance, and energy consumption while preserving flexibility for embedded applications.

title: A High-Speed Image Acquisition System Based on State Machine and Fast ADCs
journal: IEEE Nuclear Science Symposium and Medical Imaging Conference.
abstract: Abstract—This work presents the development of a digital system for image acquisition capable of processing two electrical signals with amplitudes ranging from 0 to 10 V. The system correlates both signals to generate a two-dimensional histogram, where the X and Y coordinates of each event are derived from the amplitudes of the two coincident signals. The hardware consists of two analog-to-digital converters (ADCs), control electronics, and a 1M static random access memory (SRAM), implemented on a card that can be connected to a personal computer via an ISA bus. The system achieves a data acquisition rate of up to 1.0 × 10⁶ events per second, independent of the PC processor. The software was developed in the Delphi environment using Assembly routines. Image sizes can be configured from 128 × 128 to 1024 × 1024 pixels and may be visualized using three-dimensional graphics. Experimental results demonstrate the applicability of the system to two-dimensional position-sensitive X-ray detectors.

title: A TDC-Based System for X-Ray Imaging Detectors
journal: IEEE Nuclear Science Symposium and Medical Imaging Conference.
abstract: This work presents an image acquisition system designed for use with position-sensitive detectors (PSD). The system is based on a high-resolution time-to-digital converter (TDC) and a field-programmable gate array (FPGA). The detectors use gas as the absorbing medium and employ two delay lines to determine the coordinates of each detected particle. By reading the position information provided by the delay lines, the system encodes the incidence position of each event within the detector window. The TDC converts the timing information from the delay lines into digital data, while the FPGA processes each event, controls system operation, and manages communication with a personal computer. Images with a resolution of 256 × 256 pixels are stored in onboard memory, and this resolution can be increased to 512 × 512 pixels through a time-multiplexing technique. The system supports a maximum data acquisition rate of 1.2 million events per second.

title: Detecção de Raios Cósmicos Usando Filtragem Ótima
authors: F. Marroquim L. de A. Jr
journal: XXIV Encontro Nacional de Física de Partículas e Campos
abstract: Raios cósmicos de alta energia são núcleos acelerados que atingem energias cinéticas superiores a 10²⁰ eV. A natureza desse processo de aceleração permanece como uma das grandes questões da física, motivando o desenvolvimento de instrumentos modernos para sua detecção e estudo. O projeto MARIACHI (Mixed Apparatus for RADAR Investigation of Cosmic Ray of High Ionization), localizado no Laboratório Nacional de Brookhaven (BNL), busca detectar raios cósmicos de altíssimas energias utilizando a técnica de espalhamento frontal de ondas eletromagnéticas. Esse método, amplamente empregado na detecção de meteoros, pode ser adaptado para raios cósmicos, pois, assim como os meteoros, esses eventos são capazes de ionizar átomos da atmosfera, refletindo ondas eletromagnéticas na faixa de VHF. Os sinais refletidos são captados por antenas e demodulados, apresentando baixa relação sinal-ruído devido à fraca intensidade das ondas refletidas. Dessa forma, torna-se necessário o uso de técnicas avançadas de detecção e classificação de sinais em ambientes ruidosos. Este trabalho utiliza processamento estocástico baseado em filtragem ótima para a detecção automática de raios cósmicos no contexto do projeto MARIACHI. Nessa abordagem, as propriedades estatísticas do ruído aditivo e do próprio sinal são exploradas para projetar um filtro linear que maximiza a relação sinal-ruído, conhecido como filtro casado. A detecção foi realizada de forma offline a partir de dados reais digitalizados, e a implementação do filtro casado, bem como resultados relevantes, são apresentados e discutidos.

title: Test Station for the CARIOCA FE-chip of the LHCb Muon Detector
journal: 11th Workshop on Electronics for LHC and Future Experiments
abstract: This document describes the hardware and software of a Front-End Electronics Test Station developed to test and characterize the Front-End ASIC used in the LHCb Muon chambers. This ASIC is an 8-channel device that integrates amplification, shaping, discrimination, and baseline restoration, processing readout signals generated by Multi-Wire Proportional and GEM chambers operating in high-radiation environments. The design is predominantly analog. Approximately 25,000 chips are expected to be produced during the engineering and production phases, all requiring individual testing prior to final assembly on printed circuit boards. The proposed test system includes features such as variable and bipolar charge injection, custom readout, rate and timing measurement circuitry, power consumption monitoring, as well as data analysis and statistical tools. The system is controlled through a LabVIEW-based interface. Results from approximately 3,000 ASICs tested during the engineering run are presented, demonstrating the effectiveness of the test platform.

title: A ROOT Tool for 3D Event Visualization in ATLAS Calorimeter
journal: XI International Workshop on Advanced Computing and Analysis Techniques in Physics Research
abstract: The ATLAS (A Toroidal LHC Apparatus) detector was tested with cosmic rays prior to the start of full operation of the Large Hadron Collider (LHC) in 2008. During the commissioning phase, the development of specialized tools for efficient cosmic ray data analysis is essential. A key requirement for final analysis is the ability to visualize cosmic muon tracks and the corresponding activated cells in the detector, enabling visual verification of data coherence and identification of potential issues. This work presents a 3D visualization tool for cosmic muon track reconstruction based on activated cells in the highly segmented ATLAS calorimeter system. The tool was developed using the ROOT framework, allowing seamless integration with analysis tools used by the ATLAS community. It employs the ROOT embedded geometry package to model the calorimeter structure at the cell level and provides routines to populate cells with reconstructed data. User-defined energy thresholds can be applied to display only relevant information. The structure of the tool and application examples using reconstructed data are presented.

title: A Matched Filter for Cosmic Ray Detection from Electromagnetic Wave Reflection
journal: XI International Workshop on Advanced Computing and Analysis Techniques in Physics Research
abstract: A new technique for detecting high-energy cosmic rays based on the forward scattering of electromagnetic waves has recently been introduced. In this approach, reflected signals are captured by antennas and subsequently demodulated. This work employs optimal filtering to detect cosmic ray events and suppress the significant background noise present in the system. The proposed detection method achieves a detection efficiency of 98% while maintaining a false alarm probability as low as 1.0%.

title: Combining Hough Transform and Optimal Filtering for Efficient Cosmic Ray Detection with a Hadronic Calorimeter
journal: XII Advanced Computing and Analysis Techniques in Physics Research
abstract: The hadronic calorimeter of ATLAS, TileCal, comprises a large number of readout channels (approximately 10,000), enabling the detection of tracks when cosmic muons traverse the detector. Muon track detection has been widely used during the TileCal commissioning phase for energy and timing calibration and remains important for background noise suppression during standard LHC operation. This work presents a cosmic ray detection algorithm based on TileCal data. The method employs the Hough Transform to map activated calorimeter cell data into a parameter space corresponding to straight-line tracks, where detection is efficiently performed. Due to the inherently low signal-to-noise ratio in cosmic ray detection with TileCal, a preprocessing stage based on a matched filter applied to time-sampled signals is introduced to enhance detection performance. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms previously used straight-line fitting approaches in TileCal.

title: Detecção de Raios Cósmicos por Calorímetros Finamente Segmentados Usando a Transformada de Hough
journal: XV Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: In High Energy Physics experiments, calorimeters are responsible for absorbing and measuring the energy of particles as they traverse the detector material. Modern calorimeters feature a large number of readout channels, and their highly segmented design also enables track detection. This work presents a cosmic ray detection algorithm for a hadronic calorimeter (TileCal) based on the Hough Transform. The algorithm uses track information derived from activated calorimeter cells when particles such as muons cross the detector. Details of the proposed method and experimental results are presented. Due to the low signal-to-noise ratio of TileCal for cosmic ray detection, the proposed approach demonstrates significantly higher efficiency compared to traditional line-fitting algorithms, such as least-squares methods.

title: Cosmic Ray Detection from Electromagnetic Wave Reflection Using a Matched Filter
journal: 6th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Computing Science and Automatic Control
abstract: A new technique for detecting high-energy cosmic rays based on the forward scattering of electromagnetic waves has recently been introduced. In this approach, reflected signals are captured by antennas and subsequently demodulated. This work employs optimal filtering to detect cosmic ray events and suppress the significant background noise present in the system. The proposed detection system achieves a detection efficiency of 98% with a false alarm probability as low as 1%.

title: Sistema Detector para Calorimetria de Altas Energias em Condições de Baixa Relação Sinal-Ruído
journal: XVIII Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: Este artigo apresenta os resultados do desenvolvimento de um receptor de sinais para o sistema de filtragem on-line de um detector de partículas que opera em um ambiente de alta taxa de eventos. No sistema de calorimetria, responsável pela medição de energia, o receptor processa sinais de partículas de interesse, como o múon, que apresentam uma baixa relação sinal-ruído. Para melhorar essa relação, o sistema realiza a soma analógica de dois canais de leitura, além de compatibilizar o sistema de calorimetria com o detector específico de múons e enviar o sinal de decisão para o sistema de filtragem on-line. O receptor também utiliza um filtro capaz de compensar o efeito da limitação de banda dos cabos de transmissão de sinal, que possuem 70 metros de comprimento. Como resultado, é alcançada uma eficiência de detecção de 90%, com uma taxa de falso alarme de 10%, utilizando-se um detector por patamar.

title: Optimal Signal Selection for a Highly Segmented Detector
journal: 39th International Congress and Exposition on Noise Control Engineering.
abstract: This work presents an extensive study of signal detection in the presence of noise for a high-energy calorimeter used in particle collider experiments. The objective is to select the calorimeter cells, out of approximately 10,000 available readout channels—most of which contain no signal—that should be considered for energy reconstruction. To achieve this, several signal detection techniques are explored, including Maximum Likelihood, independent component analysis, and neural processing. The results indicate that the neural network approach outperforms the other methods, both in terms of detection performance and implementation complexity.

title: A Low-Cost Implementation of High-Order Square M-QAM Detection/Demodulation in a FPGA Device
journal: 7th International Telecommunications Symposium
abstract: This paper outlines a low-cost technique for detection and demodulation of high-order square M-ary quadrature amplitude modulation (M-QAM), with M = 4, 16, 64, 256, 1024, and 4096, implemented in a field programmable gate array (FPGA) device. This approach is particularly relevant for advancing high-speed power line communication technology. The proposed technique is based on a heuristic decision region approach combined with a nonlinear function, which simplifies the hardware implementation of detection and demodulation of high-order square M-QAM symbols. The performance evaluation is conducted considering channels with additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) and additive impulsive Gaussian noise (AIGN). The results, in terms of bit error rate versus energy per bit to noise power spectral density ratio (Eb/N0), indicate that the proposed technique achieves reduced performance losses. Additionally, its FPGA implementation requires low hardware resources and presents low latency.

title: Implementação em FPGA de Detectores Baseados em Filtragem Casada e Redes Neurais em Ambientes de Física Experimental de Altas Energias
journal: XIX Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: This work presents an implementation of two detection algorithms in FPGA in an experimental high energy physics environment, namely the hadronic calorimeter (TileCal) of the ATLAS detector at CERN. It focuses on the design of detectors based on a matched filter and an artificial neural network. The results show that the neural detector outperforms the matched filter for the considered application.

title: Projeto e Implementação de um Sistema de Detecção com Probabilidade de Falso Alarme Ajustável
journal: XIX Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: This work proposes an implementation of a robust signal-to-noise detection system that ensures a constant level of contamination based on an adaptive threshold, even when the contamination level changes according to a set of adopted constraints. Traditional signal detection methods assume a constant contamination level to classify events; however, since the scenario may vary, these methods can lead to higher false alarm rates as the contamination level changes. By monitoring the noise variance in real time, the proposed method implements a trigger system in which the decision threshold is proportional to the noise standard deviation, thereby ensuring a constant false alarm rate. The FPGA implementation of the algorithm is presented, along with simulation results for an optimal signal detector.

title: Sistema Digital para Redução do Empilhamento em Medições de Energia num Detector de Partículas
journal: XX Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: ATLAS has been developed as a general-purpose particle detector and is located at one of the collision points of the LHC (Large Hadron Collider) at CERN. Currently, the LHC and its detectors are undergoing an extensive upgrade program to enable increases in collision energy and luminosity (event rate). This new scenario presents challenges for detector electronic readout systems due to increased detector occupancy, which results in pileup noise. In ATLAS, calorimeters play a major role by measuring the energy of incoming particles and assisting in the particle identification process, which is crucial for event selection. The ATLAS calorimeters comprise more than 100,000 readout channels, and as pileup increases, particle detection efficiency is significantly affected. The purpose of this work is to propose an embedded electronic system for pileup compensation in the ATLAS calorimeter system. The approach employs a bank of filters implemented using FPGA technology, and promising results are demonstrated using ATLAS simulation data.

title: Reconstrução Online para Calorímetros Operando em Condições de Altas Luminosidades
journal: XX Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: This paper presents an online energy reconstruction algorithm for calorimeters operating in high-luminosity environments, along with its FPGA implementation. Based on the Gradient Descent algorithm, the proposed method has its properties defined through the COF (Constrained Optimal Filter) approach, which aims to design a luminosity-independent signal amplitude estimator. In addition, COF enables the recovery of both the central signal amplitude and the amplitudes of each superposed component. However, the method involves matrix inversion, which is computationally expensive. To address this, the Gradient Descent algorithm is applied to the COF method, reducing the computations to sums and products, which are more suitable for FPGA-based online applications. To support this approach, a dedicated RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) processor with a pipelined architecture was developed to optimize FPGA resource usage. Additionally, a C compiler was created to facilitate the development of Gradient Descent algorithm subroutines.

title: Implementação em FPGA de Transformada Wavelet para Compactação de Sinais Elétricos de Sistemas de Potência Utilizando Processador Embarcado
journal: XX Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: This work aims to present an implementation of the Wavelet Transform on an FPGA (Field Programmable Gate Array). Using this tool, it is possible to achieve fast compression of electrical signals, either smoothing or enhancing their details. Additionally, the work compares this approach when implemented on an embedded processor with a direct implementation that uses multiple logic blocks, referred to as the parallel method. The comparison considers both the number of logic blocks required and the processing speed in each case. A prototype was developed to implement both methods, successfully achieving signal compression in which more than 85% of the samples, acquired at a rate of 7.68 kHz, could be replaced by zero while preserving over 99.9% of the original signal energy. The reconstruction error was limited to approximately 10⁻²%.

title: Método de Reamostragem Aplicado ao Esquema de Grupo da Norma IEC 61000-4-7
journal: XX Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: Harmonics and interharmonics detection is typically performed using the FFT (Fast Fourier Transform), along with the IEC 61000-4-7 grouping and subgrouping methods, which aim to cluster the spread energy in the frequency spectrum. However, for signals with deviations in the fundamental frequency, this approach becomes less efficient. Therefore, this work presents a time-domain interpolation technique that synchronizes the signal prior to the application of the FFT.

title: Comparison of interpolation methods in time and frequency domain for the estimation of harmonics and interharmonics according to IEC standard
journal: 16th International Conference on Harmonics and Quality of Power
abstract: The Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) is the most commonly used technique for analyzing power system signals. However, when the signal is not synchronized or when interharmonics are present, spectral leakage can occur. To address this issue, several techniques have been proposed in the literature. This paper presents a comparison of results for calculating harmonics and interharmonics groups and subgroups according to IEC 61000-4-7, using two of these techniques: one based on frequency-domain interpolation and the other based on time-domain interpolation.

title: An Electrical Signal Disturbance Detector and Compressor Based on FPGA Platform
journal: 16th International Conference on Harmonics and Quality of Power
abstract: This paper presents a prototype system for detecting and compressing disturbances commonly found in electrical signals, in both voltage and current. The detection and compression algorithms are implemented on an FPGA platform. The compression process operates in three stages: the innovation stage, the wavelet stage, and the bit compression stage, which together provide an efficient compression rate. In addition to describing the algorithm, the paper presents details of the hardware implementation and results obtained from both synthesized and real signals.

title: Correção Não Linear na Estimação de Energia para um Calorímetro de Altas Energias
journal: 12º Congresso Brasileiro de Inteligência Computacional
abstract: Em ambientes de alta taxa de eventos, a resposta do experimento pode ser distorcida devido à presença de informação proveniente de eventos adjacentes. Esse efeito de empilhamento de sinais degrada a eficiência de métodos lineares utilizados para estimar os parâmetros de interesse. No contexto da física de altas energias, a energia do sinal de interesse é medida por meio da estimativa da amplitude do pulso recebido do sistema de calorimetria. Atualmente, o LHC (Large Hadron Collider) opera em uma taxa de eventos que produz empilhamento de sinais. Este trabalho apresenta um método alternativo para estimar a energia das células do Calorímetro de Telhas (TileCal) do detector ATLAS no LHC nessas condições de empilhamento. A abordagem proposta utiliza uma técnica linear baseada no estimador de máxima verossimilhança combinada com uma rede neural. A rede neural tem a função de corrigir as não linearidades introduzidas pela estatística do empilhamento de sinais na estimativa linear. Os resultados mostram que o uso da abordagem proposta melhora consideravelmente a resolução de energia em condições de operação em alta taxa.

title: Estimativa da Energia do Sinal de Múons do Calorímetro Hadrônico do Experimento ATLAS.
journal: XXXIII Simpósio Brasileiro de Telecomunicações
abstract: Este trabalho apresenta o estudo, desenvolvimento e implementação da técnica mais eficiente para estimativa de energia no contexto de um projeto relacionado à física experimental de altas energias. Técnicas de estimação encontradas na literatura foram aplicadas no contexto do projeto e comparadas. Os resultados, para dados simulados e experimentais, mostraram que o estimador de amplitude de pulso de máxima verossimilhança para o caso de ruído gaussiano atinge a melhor eficiência em termos de erro de estimação, juntamente com a menor complexidade computacional, sendo o mais indicado para a aplicação.

title: Estudos sobre a implementação online para estimativa de energia no TileCal em cenários de alta luminosidade.
authors: João Paulo B. da S. Duarte
journal: XXI Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: This work aims to study techniques for online energy estimation in the ATLAS hadronic calorimeter (TileCal) at the LHC collider. During future periods of LHC operation, signals from adjacent collisions will be observed within the same time window, leading to signal superposition. In this environment, the COF (Constrained Optimal Filter) energy reconstruction method outperforms the algorithm currently implemented in the system. However, the COF method requires matrix inversion, making its online implementation infeasible. To overcome this limitation, this work presents iterative methods to implement COF, resulting in simpler mathematical operations. Based on Gradient Descent, the proposed algorithms demonstrate the ability to estimate the amplitude of superimposed signals with efficiency comparable to COF.

title: Detecção de Sinal em Calorimetria de Altas Energias Utilizando Redes Neurais Artificiais
authors: João Paulo Bittencourt da Silveira Duarte
journal: XXI Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: Signal superposition is a recurrent problem when information is transmitted through a communication channel at a rate higher than its response time. Modern particle colliders, operating with collision rates of tens of millions of subatomic particles per second, exhibit signal superposition in their front-end electronics, which hinders the selection of events of interest by the detector’s trigger systems. Current deconvolution methods model the front-end electronics as a linear transmission channel whose transfer function can be compensated using linear channel equalization techniques, allowing the cancellation of intersymbol interference and proper interpretation of previously superimposed data. Optimal approximations of this equalizer are typically implemented using FIR filters. However, this linear compensation approach does not account for intrinsic nonlinearities present in sensor outputs. In this work, instead of using linear channel equalization, a neural network (NN) classifier is employed to perform the detection of the signal of interest. Simulations are used to compare the detection efficiency of both methods under different event rate scenarios.

title: Sistema para Redução da Sobreposição de Sinais no Calorímetro Hadrônico do Detector ATLAS
journal: XXI Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: The LHC particle accelerator will soon undergo an upgrade process in which parameters such as collision energy and the number of particle interactions will be increased. This new scenario will directly impact detector instrumentation systems. For the ATLAS detector, a key issue arising after this upgrade in its energy measurement subsystem (the calorimeter) is the superimposition of information from consecutive events, a signal distortion known as pile-up. This work proposes a dedicated signal processing system embedded in a high-performance reconfigurable digital platform to reduce pile-up effects in the ATLAS hadronic calorimeter. The approach uses a parallel implementation of digital finite impulse response (FIR) filters, where each filter approximates the inverse of the measurement channel impulse response, effectively performing signal deconvolution. The results demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed solution and its compliance with the time latency requirements of the application.

title: Técnicas de Deconvolução Aplicadas à Estimação de Energia Online em Calorimetria de Altas Energias em Condições de Alta Taxa de Eventos
journal: XXI Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: This work presents a study of deconvolution techniques that can be applied to online energy reconstruction in the ATLAS hadronic calorimeter (TileCal) first-level trigger system. The high-rate event environment expected in future LHC operations increases the likelihood of adjacent collisions, leading to signal superposition. The current energy reconstruction algorithm is not robust against this pile-up effect. To address this issue, several deconvolution methods are investigated, including two direct approaches that result in FIR filters and three iterative techniques based on Gradient Descent, which allow the incorporation of prior knowledge. As expected, the iterative methods achieve lower estimation errors compared to the direct FIR approaches. However, in terms of implementation, the iterative techniques are more complex and require greater hardware resources.

title: A Gapless Waveform Recorder for Monitoring Smart Grids
journal: 17th International Conference on Harmonics and Quality of Power
abstract: In the present paper a new concept of continuous waveform recording, or gapless waveform recording, applied to Electric Power System (EPS) signals is presented. It is shown that it is not necessary to store all the EPS signal samples, and still, keeping all the relevant information for a complete reconstruction of the signal for later oscillographic analysis. This allows store long periods of waveform measurements, spending little memory space. To make it possible, it is used the Novelty Detection Concept, that is performed by segmenting the signal into frames and comparing these frames in order to save only the ones in which some “novelty” is present. This approach contributes to the new context of Smart Grids (SGs), even in a future scenario where events that are not completely known might occur, since all the necessary waveform information is saved. Thus, the control system of the SG can use the stored signals to perform different analysis and take the suitable decision to improve the EPS performance.

title: The Concept of Novelty Detection Applied to Power Quality
journal: EEE Power & Energy Society General Meeting
abstract: This paper presents a study on a possible topology of a novelty detector to be applied in Power Systems applications. In the context of Smart Grids, a novelty detector should not be limited to typical power quality (PQ) disturbances, but rather be capable of identifying any new deviation or variation in the signal. Therefore, traditional novelty detection methodologies based on a training stage are not suitable. The proposed detection approach divides the signal into fixed-length frames and relies on comparing the current frame with a reference frame using a specific metric. Results for two scenarios are presented, achieving a hit rate of up to 99.0% with a false alarm rate of 1.0%.

title: Implementação em FPGA de um Algoritmo para Análise de Variações de Tensão segundo a Norma IEC 61000-4-30
journal: XII Conferência Brasileira sobre Qualidade da Energia Elétrica
abstract: O presente trabalho apresenta o desenvolvimento, implementação e teste de um coprocessador utilizado para a implementação de um algoritmo de detecção e classificação de distúrbios de variação de tensão, como afundamento (sag), elevação (swell) e interrupção. O algoritmo foi desenvolvido de acordo com os requisitos do padrão classe A, segundo a norma IEC 61000-4-30. A implementação do detector é realizada por meio de um sistema de processadores embarcados em uma plataforma FPGA (Field Programmable Gate Array), criada e idealizada para aplicações de processamento digital de sinais. Os resultados foram obtidos por meio de testes utilizando o gerador de sinais específico para sistemas de potência OMICRON CMC 256 Plus, que possui uma função para geração de distúrbios de qualidade de energia.

title: Energy Estimation Considerations for a High-Energy Calorimeter Operating at High Pile-Up Conditions
journal: XX Encontro Nacional de Modelagem Computacional
abstract: The high-luminosity LHC program introduces new challenges to calorimeter energy reconstruction. Due to the increased number of interactions per collision, the calorimeter readout signal may be distorted by pile-up effects, which introduce nonlinearities into the readout channel noise and degrade the performance of standard energy estimation algorithms. This work evaluates the performance of two promising methods recently proposed to address the pile-up effect in high-energy calorimetry. The first method is based on a signal deconvolution technique, while the second combines a linear approach with a neural network that refines the linear estimation. Both methods are developed for the ATLAS Tile Calorimeter, currently in operation at the LHC. A simulation dataset was generated covering a wide range of pile-up scenarios expected for the high-luminosity LHC. The results show that energy estimation efficiency can be significantly improved.

title: Representação Esparsa Aplicada à Estimação Online de Energia para um Calorímetro Submetido a uma Alta Taxa de Eventos
authors: João Paulo Bittencourt da Silveira Duarte
journal: XXXV Simpósio Brasileiro de Telecomunicações e Processamento de Sinais
abstract: Este trabalho apresenta um estudo sobre técnicas de equalização de canal aplicadas à reconstrução online de energia nos calorímetros do ATLAS (A Toroidal LHC ApparatuS). O ambiente de alta taxa de eventos, previsto para os próximos anos no colisionador de partículas LHC (Large Hadron Collider), aumenta a probabilidade de ocorrência de colisões adjacentes, produzindo o efeito de empilhamento de sinais. O algoritmo atualmente utilizado para a reconstrução não é robusto a esse efeito. Técnicas de deconvolução já foram propostas em outros trabalhos, e métodos baseados em filtros FIR (Finite Impulse Response) obtiveram resultados superiores à técnica atual. Este trabalho propõe o uso de uma técnica baseada em representações esparsas, cujos resultados apontam melhora na estimação em relação à aplicação de filtros FIR.

title: Redes Neurais para Filtragem Inversa com Aplicação em Calorímetros Operando a Alta Taxa de Eventos
authors: Joõo Paulo B. da S. Duarte
journal: XXXV Simpósio Brasileiro de Telecomunicações e Processamento de Sinais
abstract: Este trabalho tem como objetivo a implementação de um sistema inverso para remover a resposta ao impulso de um calorímetro operando a uma alta taxa de eventos. O calorímetro TileCal apresenta uma resposta ao impulso maior do que a taxa de eventos adquiridos, gerando empilhamento de sinais na sua eletrônica de leitura. Foram propostos filtros FIR que aproximam o sistema inverso; no entanto, características não lineares intrínsecas ao calorímetro, como desvio de fase e deformação do pulso, fazem com que filtros lineares não sejam adequados a esse cenário. Devido a essas características, é proposto um filtro inverso baseado em redes neurais, implementável em hardware dedicado (FPGA). Este método mostrou-se mais eficiente quando comparado a um método de filtragem baseado em filtro linear.

title: Contribuições para o Sistema Digital de Seleção Online de Múons a Partir de Informações de Calorimetria no Detector ATLAS
journal: XXXV Simpósio Brasileiro de Telecomunicações e Processamento de Sinais
abstract: O sistema de seleção online de eventos (trigger) do detector ATLAS é dividido em três estágios sequenciais, sendo o primeiro implementado em hardware dedicado para atender às severas restrições de tempo de processamento e utilizando separadamente informações de três subdetectores (traços, calorímetro e câmara de múons). Este trabalho está inserido no contexto de um sistema eletrônico para permitir a utilização de informação do calorímetro (medidor de energia) na detecção de múons no primeiro nível de trigger do ATLAS. O artigo apresenta resultados relativos à implementação de um módulo digital para controle do fluxo de informações na placa desenvolvida.

title: Implementação de Circuitos Aritméticos em Ponto Flutuante, Utilizando Formato com Número de Bits Configurável
journal: XXII Congresso Brasileiro de Automática
abstract: A representation of real numbers in binary systems led to the establishment of floating-point numeric representations. In addition to accurately representing numeric values, efficiently performing arithmetic operations—such as addition, multiplication, division, and subtraction—between floating-point numbers, with minimal error and fast response time, is a key requirement. Currently, the IEEE 754 standard is the most widely used representation in computational systems. This work presents a simplified floating-point representation implementation in which the number of bits is parameterizable. The implemented circuits rely solely on combinational logic, aiming to optimize operation response time while allowing the number of logic elements to be adjusted according to the target application.

title: Estimação de Energia para o Calorímetro de Telhas do ATLAS Baseada na Filtragem de Wiener-Hopf
journal: XXI Encontro Nacional de Modelagem Computacional
abstract: Nos sistemas de calorimetria de altas energias, quando se opera em condições de alta luminosidade, ocorre o empilhamento de sinais. Dessa forma, um modelo determinístico do sinal, tipicamente adotado para a estimação de energia, torna-se inviável para níveis intensos de sobreposição. Este trabalho propõe um método de estimação de energia para o calorímetro de telhas do experimento ATLAS no LHC, baseado na teoria do Filtro de Wiener-Hopf, no qual não é necessário um modelo para a representação dos dados. Conjuntos de dados de simulação foram utilizados para projetar e avaliar a eficiência do método em condições de alta luminosidade. Os resultados mostram que a abordagem baseada no Filtro de Wiener-Hopf proposta neste trabalho supera o método atualmente utilizado para estimação de energia no calorímetro de telhas do ATLAS em condições severas de empilhamento de sinais.

title: Implementation of an Electrical Signal Compression System Using Sparce Representation
journal: 18th International Conference on Harmonics and Quality of Power
abstract: The storage of voltage and current signals over a period of time generates a large memory demand. Therefore, signal compression techniques have become important in this context. This paper presents an FPGA (Field Programmable Gate Array) implementation of a sparse representation algorithm using redundant dictionaries, applied to the compression of electrical signals from power systems. The representation is based on a dictionary composed of elements from the Fourier and Wavelet bases, capable of representing both stationary and transient components of electrical signals. The results are analyzed based on two parameters: the quality of the compressed signal, measured by its correlation coefficient relative to the original signal, and the number of elements in the representation, which is related to the compression ratio. The feasibility of real-time implementation is evaluated in terms of FPGA resource consumption and the required operating frequency.

title: Implementação em FPGA de um Método Recursivo de Deconvolução Aplicado em Calorímetros Operando a Alta Taxa de Eventos
journal: XXXVI Simpósio Brasileiro de Telecomunicações e Processamento de Sinais
abstract: The LHC particle accelerator has undergone an update process, leading to the effect known as pile-up (overlapping signals) in the experiment front-end electronics. Since the algorithm currently used for reconstructing the energy generated in these collisions is not tolerant to this effect, this work proposes a hardware implementation of an iterative method based on a variant of Gradient Descent that recovers the amplitude information of superimposed signals within an acquisition window. With this implementation, dozens of channels can be processed in parallel within a single FPGA, while also meeting the required operating latency constraints of the ATLAS acquisition system.

title: Circuito Digital para Redução da Sobreposição de Sinais num Detector de Partículas
journal: XXXVI Simpósio Brasileiro de Telecomunicações e Processamento de Sinais
abstract: O fenômeno da sobreposição de sinais está relacionado à taxa de ocorrência dos eventos e ao tempo de resposta dos sensores. No âmbito dos detectores de partículas, o atual sistema de instrumentação do calorímetro hadrônico do ATLAS, um dos detectores do LHC, não é eficaz na reconstrução dos sinais afetados pela sobreposição. Nesse sentido, este trabalho propõe a implementação de um circuito digital responsável pela reconstrução dos sinais provenientes desse calorímetro. A verificação funcional do circuito foi realizada por meio de um conjunto de dados simulados. A partir dos resultados, constatou-se que a solução proposta atendeu aos requisitos de funcionamento.

title: Implementação em FPGA de um Método Iterativo de Deconvolução para Operar no Sistema de Trigger do Experimento ATLAS
journal: XXII Encontro Nacional de Modelagem Computacional
abstract: Aceleradores de partículas, como o LHC, em Genebra, na Suíça, colidem partículas subatômicas cujos subprodutos (partículas fundamentais da natureza) são analisados por detectores ao redor do ponto de colisão. O LHC vem passando por um processo de atualização, no qual parâmetros como a energia das colisões e a quantidade de partículas por *bunch* são aumentados, impactando diretamente os sistemas de instrumentação dos detectores, como, por exemplo, o experimento ATLAS presente nesse acelerador. Esse aumento na probabilidade de ocorrência de colisões adjacentes produz um efeito conhecido como *pile-up* (empilhamento de sinais) na eletrônica de leitura dos subdetectores do ATLAS. Para lidar com esse novo desafio, métodos iterativos de deconvolução de sinais, baseados em representação esparsa de dados, como o SSF, vêm sendo propostos, apresentando resultados satisfatórios. No entanto, tais métodos apresentam alto custo computacional, de modo que o principal desafio atual é o desenvolvimento de algoritmos capazes de operar de forma online. Este trabalho apresenta um aprimoramento ao método SSF, permitindo reduzir a quantidade de iterações do algoritmo em uma ordem de grandeza, facilitando, assim, sua implementação em tempo real.

title: Estimação da Energia do Calorímetro de Telhas do Detector ATLAS no LHC em Condições de Empilhamento de Sinais
journal: XXII Encontro Nacional de Modelagem Computacional
abstract: Para a descoberta de novas partículas elementares, físicos e engenheiros se concentram em construir equipamentos cada vez mais sofisticados. Nesses sistemas, feixes de partículas conhecidas são colocados em rota de colisão a velocidades próximas à da luz. Quando ocorrem colisões, partículas elementares são produzidas e sua ocorrência é medida por um complexo arranjo de sensores denominados calorímetros. Para essa detecção, um sistema eletrônico é construído a fim de formar um pulso de natureza elétrica, cujo formato é proporcional à energia da partícula gerada. Nesse contexto, para determinar o resultado de uma determinada colisão, são propostos estimadores baseados em algoritmos matemáticos. Um dos maiores colisionadores de feixes é o LHC, que abriga o experimento ATLAS. Assim, o presente trabalho tem como objetivo avaliar o desempenho de dois algoritmos utilizados nesse experimento: o OF (Optimal Filter) e o COF (Constrained Optimal Filter), ambos projetados para o ATLAS. Para avaliar a eficiência da estimação de energia, foram utilizados dados adquiridos durante a operação nominal do LHC. Os parâmetros de média e desvio padrão do erro de estimação foram empregados para comparar o desempenho de ambos os métodos. Os resultados mostram que o método COF apresenta desempenho superior ao método OF, atualmente utilizado no experimento ATLAS.

title: Estimação da Amplitude Utilizando o Estimador de Máxima Verossimilhança em Condições de Empilhamento de Sinais
journal: XXII Encontro Nacional de Modelagem Computacional
abstract: Sistemas de reconstrução de sinais se apoiam na estimação de parâmetros a partir de amostras temporais recebidas. Em calorimetria de altas energias, o sinal é conformado de modo que seu formato seja fixo e a amplitude corresponda ao parâmetro a ser estimado. Tipicamente, métodos lineares são empregados, visto que o ruído eletrônico presente no sinal recebido pode ser modelado por uma função Gaussiana. Entretanto, em operações com alta taxa de eventos, como as presentes no LHC, no CERN, o sinal recebido é deformado pelo efeito de empilhamento de sinais, comprometendo a eficiência de métodos lineares. Dessa forma, este trabalho avalia o uso de estimadores baseados na teoria da máxima verossimilhança (MLE), em que o ruído é descrito por funções multivariadas Gaussiana e Lognormal. Para comparar a eficiência, foi gerado um conjunto de dados contendo diferentes condições de empilhamento de sinais, considerando o calorímetro de telhas (TileCal) do ATLAS no LHC. Os resultados mostram que os métodos baseados em MLE apresentam melhores eficiências quando comparados ao método atualmente utilizado no TileCal.

title: Combinando Filtro Casado e Topologia para Aprimoramento da Reconstrução de Múons no Calorímetro Hadrônico do ATLAS
journal: XXXVII Simpósio Brasileiro de Telecomunicações e Processamento de Sinais
abstract: Este trabalho apresenta o estudo de técnicas de discriminação de eventos de partículas do experimento ATLAS. O estimador de energia de máxima verossimilhança Gaussiano (MLEG) foi aplicado no contexto deste projeto e comparado com a técnica usada atualmente no calorímetro, o Filtro Ótimo (OF2). Diferentes topologias de fusão da informação de interesse também foram avaliadas.
